chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: How exactly import works in python  How you guys when you import a package  So its functions are available in your program  In today's video we are going to see this thing  Let's go to the middle screen

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: And let's get started  So whenever we import anything in the python program  We have imported a module, imported anything  So all that code from where you have imported  Comes in our main program

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Let me show you what I am talking about  First of all, you see here I have written  RAPL in which I have told you  What is importing and how it works  Importing in python is a process of loading code

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: From a python module into the current script  Current script is like my main.py  Okay  I have written import pandas here  Pandas has been imported

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: All methods and classes of pandas  I can use by typing pandas.  I can write here pandas.readcsv  And I can use this function  Okay

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: I can use pandas.readxl function  And so on  Okay  So I can do this thing  And after readcsv if I give any csv file argument

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Then what it does is it reads csv as a data frame  That's a matter of later  But I just want to tell you that by importing pandas  All the code of pandas has become accessible for me  I should say

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Now see here  As well as any additional module  That depends on the imported module  If pandas depends on date utils  Then date utils will also be available

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Like pandas  Okay  Now see one more thing here  To import a module in python  You can use import statement

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Followed by the name of the module  As we have done so far  For example to import the math module  Which contains a variety of mathematical functions  Now import math

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Let's take the example of math  Because it's a little easy  Okay  Allow  Import math

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: And I will write here  Math.  And here I have got all the functions of math  Okay  If you are in any decent editor

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Then it will give you suggestions  If you are using an applet  It will give you suggestions  Okay  So if I run this

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Then in math  I can use the function called floor  In fact, any function in math  Which is present in math  I can use that

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: By using the math.syntax  Okay, so this is one thing  Now here I will tell you the story ahead  Once the module is imported  You can use any of the function and variable

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Defined in the module  By using the dot notation  Like here I have shown you using sqrt  Math.sqrt9  Which will return 3 here

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: So let's do this example  I will show you  I will run it  So 3 will be printed  No surprises there

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: I have imported it twice  No problem  Now here what is the from keyword  Now suppose I want to import only one function  Of the name sqrt

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: So I will write from math  From math  Import  From math  Import

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: sqrt  Of pi  Okay  sqrt of pi  Now I will not do math.

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Now I can directly use sqrt  And I can also use pi  So from math I have taken out sqrt  And pi  Okay

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Now I can do here  Multiply by pi  Which is here  Equal to 3.14 something  And here see it gave me 9.42

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: 3.14  Multiply by 3  Which is here 9.42 something  Something is coming  Okay, so I did this thing

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: I used the value of pi  Sqrt was a function  I used that too  Both are imported  Now there is one more thing

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Which you can do  That is  From math  Import star  By importing star

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: What will be done by python  Math.py  Which is an inbuilt module  Inside that all functions  And variables

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Which are present here  Will be made available  Now here you see  That it is showing me some error  It is saying

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Unable to detect and define names  Actually linting is also not possible  By pylint  Because there are many things  Which are imported

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: But I can use sqrt and pi  This is not a recommended approach  Why this is not a recommended approach  Because we are importing everything  All things are imported from math

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Inside our script  So see it is also possible  To import all functions and variables  From a module using star while cut  However

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: This is generally not recommended  Because it can lead to confusion  And make it harder to understand  Where specific functions and variables  Are coming from

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: So in general it happens  That if you  Start from math import  And say there is a module named harry  And you start from harry import

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Both have a function named pi  Or a constant named pi  So it will be a problem  So you will not want this  So you systematically

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Import the things you want  From the module you want  This is recommended  I am not saying this will not work  I am not saying this

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: You will not be able to see  I am not saying this is wrong  Recommended approach is  If you are a beginner  You don't know what you are doing

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: You should not start from math import  But from math import  Whatever function like sqrt  Or whatever variable like pi  Whatever you want to use

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: You should do that  Now there is an as keyword  What does this as keyword do  If I say  Like import pandas as pd

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Or import numpy as np  So that it can be written in short form  pd.read csv  pd.write csv  By doing this

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: In the same way from math  Import  I can do something like this  sqrt  pi

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Or as  Or I will do sqrt  As s  So I can import it as s  I can do something like this

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: I can do something like this  Import pi and sqrt  As s  So the sqrt will be as s  So I will use it as s

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: And pi as it is  Will be imported  Similarly I can do something like this  Import math as m  And then I will

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: m.sqrt  And m.pi  I will comment it  m.pi  Something like this

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: m.pi  Then my result will be the same  9.42 something  So I can use as keyword  So that I don't have to write the names of big models

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: If I import tensorflow as tf  Then I don't have to write tensorflow.tensorflow.  Again and again  I can refer tensorflow as tf.  So here

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: A very good application comes  As keyword when  The name of the module is big  I hope that helps. Sometimes the name of the function is big  Still you want to import it as

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Sometimes you do this purposefully  Because your  Function is a little  You want to make it more explanatory  Suppose you want this program to be understood by more people

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: So you can say  Import math as  Built in  Python  So suppose you want to

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Explain it more like this  That this is a math module which is built in  Python  So you can use this kind of name  Now I will show you the use case of a dir function

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: What is dir function  Suppose you  Don't know about any module  I will comment it  And suppose I

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Import math  So I can print all functions of math  And what are the things in it  I can print all this by using  Dir function

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: So if I do dir math, then something like this will be printed  Now see what is happening here  It is telling me what functions are there  And variables are present  Like if you see here, you will get to see pi

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Here is pi  You will get to see all these functions  Whatever things are being exported in it  Coming here means  Whatever things are being imported here

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: You will get to see  So you can see  What are the functions in it  What are the variables  Suppose I want to see

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: What is math.nan  So I can print math.nan  So I will print it  Or I can print its type  Type of math.nan

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: What is its type  Is it a string, is it a number  What is it, so it is a class float  It is not a number, it is a class float  So I hope

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: You have understood this thing  That these are different functions  And methods that are present in it  I can see that, similarly if I  Install tensorflow, then I can see it in tensorflow

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Similarly if I install flask  Then I can see it in flask and so on  So it works like this  Now I would like to show you one more thing  I would like to make a file here

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: I will name it harry.py  I will write a function here  I will write a function  I will write a function named welcome  And I will print here

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Hey you are welcome my friend  Ok  And I will write a variable here  I will write here  Harry is equal to a good boy

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Ok  Now what I will do  I will say from harry  Import  Welcome

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: And what else I will import  Harry, I will import both these things  Now what is it saying  It is saying  Harry.welcome imported but unused

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Ok, we will use it  I will use welcome  And I will print  Harry  And you can see

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: That this was the module written by me  I used it  Here you can see, hey you are welcome my friend  Which was produced by this function  And I printed

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Harry, a good boy  I made a variable in harry.py  I can print it here  If I would have written from harry  Import star, it would have worked

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: No problem, all things are imported from harry  From harry  Import star  What is being done in this process  Harry file is being imported

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Harry from harry  Import welcome harry  These two things are from harry  From harry.py and are being imported  And I can use it

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: So this is all  I hope you understood this  Hey you are welcome my friend and a good boy  These two things are being printed  But if I

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Import star as s  Will it work  No, it won't  What is to be imported  No, it won't

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: From harry, if I say import  Harry as hr  So I can use it  I can't use welcome and harry as it is  Why

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Because I have to do it by hr.  Because I have done as hr  So I have to use welcome function from harry  I have to use harry variable from harry  So I have to do hr.

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: Because I have imported harry as hr  Hope that makes sense  I will run it and show you  It will run too  So this was

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: How to import module in a program  How does importing work  I hope you understood  And practice it more  You will understand things

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: And I don't think it's a big rocket science  As you  Will do python programming  You will find things more easy  And as you will import

chunks.title: How import works in Python
chunks.text: You will know  What is coming from which file  So that you can do debugging well  How does importing work  I hope you enjoyed
